b: ^Man, this car fucking sucks. I should totally get a Chinese EV...
^Heat waves emanate from the hood of my car like a hot summer day in Helsingborg.

c-0: ^The hood of the car slides open.
c-1: ^I don't open the hood. For whatever reason. I can always come back when I feel like taking this seriously.

b: ^The coolant reservoir is empty. I should check the perimeter of the car.

EngineState: ^The engine looks like a jumbled mess of wires and metal. Some people know what this all means and how to fix it.

c-0: ^I check the spolarvätska fluid. It's half full, doesn't seem to be the issue.
c-1: ^I pull the dipstick out, methodically wipe it on my coat sleeve, and dip it once more. A bit low, but not enough to kill the engine.
c-2: ^I lean over the car and look at an empty reservoir. It is dry as dust. It's usually a lot more blue.

b: ^Maybe it has to do with the piss puddle I saw behind the car. I should look at it again.
^If the fluid is gone, it had to leak out somewhere. I should check around the car.
^I need to find something to plug the hole, something to bind it tight, and some fluid to replace what I lost. Time to lock in.

InvestigateHole: ^I already know the coolant leaked out. Now that I've seen the puddle, I know exactly where to look.

c-0: ^Running my hand along the coolant hose, my thumb catches on a massive puncture.
^I don't have any spare coolant or real tools with me. However, only city detectives need gimmicks, country detectives make do.
^I should search inside the car and around the roadside. I need to find something to plug the hole, something to wrap it with, and some liquid to replace the coolant.

tutorial: ^That's enough information to build a profile. Whenever I piece together a major lead or inspect key evidence, a new "Suspect" will unlock on my Clueboard.
^My first suspect? This busted engine. Now, I need to find 3 items to patch the leak, open the trunk, and tie them to the Engine with red string.

EvaluateSolution: ^Like my grandma used to say: It's all shits and giggles 'til someone giggles and shits. Let's see if my solution holds up.

b: ^Well, I'll be damned. It actually works.
^Gum in the hole, evidence tape wrapped tight, and the reservoir topped off with stale water.
^...I'm so smart and cool. Time to hit the road.

^I'm such a fraud. This isn't working at all! ^I need to go back to my clueboard and rethink those connections. ^Lucky for me, this is just a busted engine. I can rethink this mess and try again. At a real crime scene, I'd only get one shot at the clueboard.
^This is getting embarrassing. Let's try another combination.
^Hey you behind the computer screen! ^Genuine question. Did your mom drop you as a child?
